languages.text: {n}Эоганн нежно, кончиками пальцев, словно опасаясь, что машина и ее содержимое рассыпется в труху, касается сацеллума.{/n} "Вот мы и встретились, дражайший коллега. Я бы отдал все, чтобы твои глаза сейчас смотрели на меня с упреком, негодованием, даже ненавистью. Но я вижу в них только боль и жажду. Это жажда жизни или небытия? Я не знаю. Я давно перестал понимать себя — безумие и вина путают, сбивают с толку. Я привык к этому. Но то, что я перестал понимать тебя — это я не могу себе простить".

{n}Eogunn touches the sacellum delicately with the tips of his fingers, as though fearing that the machine and its contents will crumble to dust.{/n} "We meet again, dearest colleague. I would give anything to have you look at me now, your eyes brimming with reproach, resentment, even hatred. But all I see in them is pain and yearning. Is it yearning for life or for oblivion? I do not know. I stopped understanding myself long ago — madness and guilt are bewildering, they muddle the brain. I have grown accustomed to it. But that I stopped understanding you — I can never forgive myself for that."

languages.text: {n}Eogunn toca suavemente el sacelio con las puntas de los dedos, como si temiese que la máquina y su contenido se desmoronasen.{/n} «Volvemos a vernos, estimado colega. Daría cualquier cosa para que me mirases, aunque fuese con reproche, resentimiento o incluso odio. Pero lo único que hay en tu mirada es dolor y anhelo. ¿Es vida lo que anhelas, o acaso el olvido? Lo ignoro. Hace mucho que ni siquiera intento comprender lo que siento; la locura y la culpa me abruman y nublan mi mente. Ya me he acostumbrado a eso. Pero haber dejado de comprenderte a ti, eso jamás me lo perdonaré».
languages.original_text: {n}Eogunn touches the sacellum delicately with the tips of his fingers, as though fearing that the machine and its contents will crumble to dust.{/n} "We meet again, dearest colleague. I do not know. I stopped understanding myself long ago — madness and guilt are bewildering, they muddle the brain. I have grown accustomed to it. But that I stopped understanding you — I can never forgive myself for that."

Pero haber dejado de comprenderte a ti, eso jamás me lo perdonaré».

languages.text: {n}伊奥贡用指尖轻触那座神龛，小心翼翼，好像生怕那机器和里面的东西瞬间化为尘埃。{/n}“又见面了，最亲爱的同僚。我愿意付出一切，只求你此刻能看我一眼，哪怕你眼里满是指责、怨恨，乃至憎恨。可我在你眼里只看到痛苦和渴望。是渴望活着，还是渴望解脱？我看不清。我早就连自己都看不懂了——疯狂和愧疚令人迷惘，让人神志不清。我已经习惯了。可我居然连你都看不懂了……这一点让我永远无法原谅自己。”
languages.original_text: {n}Eogunn touches the sacellum delicately with the tips of his fingers, as though fearing that the machine and its contents will crumble to dust.{/n} "We meet again, dearest colleague. I would give anything to have you look at me now, your eyes brimming with reproach, resentment, even hatred. But all I see in them is pain and yearning. Is it yearning for life or for oblivion? I do not know. I stopped understanding myself long ago — madness and guilt are bewildering, they muddle the brain. I have grown accustomed to it. But that I stopped understanding you — I can never forgive myself for that."

languages.text: {n}Eogunn touche délicatement le sacellum du bout des doigts, comme s'il craignait que la machine et son contenu tombent en poussière à son contact.{/n} « Je suis de retour, mon très cher collègue. Je donnerais tout pour que tu tournes vers moi un regard débordant de reproche, de rancœur et même de haine, mais tout ce que je vois dans tes yeux, c'est la douleur et l'envie. L'envie de vivre ou de rejoindre le néant ? Je l'ignore. Voilà bien longtemps que j'ai cessé de me comprendre, sous l'emprise de la folie et de la culpabilité déroutantes qui troublent mon esprit. Je m'y suis habitué, mais s'il y a une chose que je ne me pardonnerai jamais, c'est d'avoir cessé de te comprendre, toi. »
languages.original_text: {n}Eogunn touches the sacellum delicately with the tips of his fingers, as though fearing that the machine and its contents will crumble to dust.{/n} "We meet again, dearest colleague. I would give anything to have you look at me now, your eyes brimming with reproach, resentment, even hatred. But all I see in them is pain and yearning. Is it yearning for life or for oblivion? I do not know. I stopped understanding myself long ago — madness and guilt are bewildering, they muddle the brain. I have grown accustomed to it. But that I stopped understanding you — I can never forgive myself for that."

Je donnerais tout pour que tu tournes vers moi un regard débordant de reproche, de rancœur et même de haine, mais tout ce que je vois dans tes yeux, c'est la douleur et l'envie. Je m'y suis habitué, mais s'il y a une chose que je ne me pardonnerai jamais, c'est d'avoir cessé de te comprendre, toi. »

languages.text: {n}Eogunn, sanki makine ve içindekiler toza dönüşecekmiş gibi korkarak hücreye parmak uçlarıyla nazikçe dokunuyor.{/n} "Tekrar karşılaştık pek değerli meslektaşım. Şu an bana bakabilmen için her şeyimi verirdim. Gözlerin sitem, kırgınlık, hatta nefretle dolu olsa bile. Fakat onlarda gördüğüm tek şey acı ve özlem. Bu yaşama duyulan bir özlem mi yoksa yok oluşa mı? Bilmiyorum. Kendimi anlamayı uzun zaman önce bıraktım. Delilik ve suçluluk kafa karıştırıcı olup zihni bulandırır. Buna alıştım. Ancak seni anlamayı bırakmam. Kendimi asla affedemem çünkü."
languages.original_text: {n}Eogunn touches the sacellum delicately with the tips of his fingers, as though fearing that the machine and its contents will crumble to dust.{/n} "We meet again, dearest colleague. I would give anything to have you look at me now, your eyes brimming with reproach, resentment, even hatred. But all I see in them is pain and yearning. Is it yearning for life or for oblivion? I do not know. I stopped understanding myself long ago — madness and guilt are bewildering, they muddle the brain. I have grown accustomed to it. But that I stopped understanding you — I can never forgive myself for that."

languages.text: {n}Eogunn berührt das Sacellum zärtlich mit seinen Fingern, als hätte er Angst, die Maschine und ihr Inhalt könnten zu Staub zerfallen.{/n} "So sieht man sich wieder, geschätzter Kollege. Ich würde alles für einen Blick von Euch geben, einen Blick voller Tadel, Groll, ja sogar Hass. Aber alles, was ich sehe, sind Schmerz und Sehnsucht. Aber ist es die Sehnsucht nach dem Leben oder dem Tod? Ich weiß es nicht. Ich habe vor langer Zeit aufgehört, mich selbst zu verstehen ... Wahnsinn und Schuld sind verstörend und zersetzen das Gehirn. Mittlerweile habe ich mich daran gewöhnt. Aber dass ich aufgehört habe, Euch zu verstehen ... das kann ich mir nie verzeihen."
languages.original_text: {n}Eogunn touches the sacellum delicately with the tips of his fingers, as though fearing that the machine and its contents will crumble to dust.{/n} "We meet again, dearest colleague. But that I stopped understanding you — I can never forgive myself for that."

languages.text: {n}エオガンは、機械とその中身が塵となって崩れ去ることを恐れるかのように、指先でそっと聖龕に触れる。{/n}「また会いましたね、親愛なる同僚。あなたが今、非難と、恨みと、いっそ憎しみさえもたたえた目で私を見てくれるなら、私はどんなものでも差し出すでしょう。しかし、その目に私が見出すのは、痛みと、焦がれるような思いばかり。それは生への思いなのか、それとも忘却への思いなのか――私には分かりません。私はとうの昔に、自分自身を理解することをやめてしまいました。狂気と罪悪感は人を惑わせ、脳を混乱させるのです。私はそれに慣れてしまった。しかし、あなたを理解することをやめてしまったこと――そればかりは、決して自らを赦すことができません」
languages.original_text: {n}Eogunn touches the sacellum delicately with the tips of his fingers, as though fearing that the machine and its contents will crumble to dust.{/n} "We meet again, dearest colleague. I would give anything to have you look at me now, your eyes brimming with reproach, resentment, even hatred. But all I see in them is pain and yearning. Is it yearning for life or for oblivion? I do not know. I stopped understanding myself long ago — madness and guilt are bewildering, they muddle the brain. I have grown accustomed to it. But that I stopped understanding you — I can never forgive myself for that."
